key_insights.insight: Stress is subjective and defined by when the load you perceive is too much to bear.
key_insights.quote: i would define stress as when the load that you perceive on you physically mentally emotionally or spiritually is too much for you to bear so yes it is subjective

key_insights.insight: Cortisol, the main stress hormone, leaks out of our sweat and impacts those around us, particularly those who are more junior.
key_insights.quote: cortisol is the main stress hormone and it will leak out of our sweat about this far around us, go into the skin of everybody else and it's going to impact them...usually the person that comes out feeling drained is less senior than the person that's had that effect on them.

key_insights.insight: The right eye to left eye contact (right eye looking into the left eye of another person) is statistically the most likely way to create emotional resonance and build a bond.
key_insights.quote: that right eye to left eye eye contact is the most bonding eye contact that you can have with someone.

key_insights.insight: For leaders, managing stress is not just important for themselves but also because their stress levels have a greater impact on their team than any other employee.
key_insights.quote: your stress levels as a leader, as a CEO, are going to have more impact on everybody else than the rest of the people put together, basically.

key_insights.insight: A key factor in whether people are open to accepting intuition is whether or not they need the science to explain it.
key_insights.quote: I think they're more open to accepting that it's a thing. And I believe that more men need the science to explain how intuition works.

key_insights.insight: Intuition isn't just in the brain; it's also in the gut, embodying wisdom and experience you're not always consciously aware of.
key_insights.quote: your memories and your cognition and your IQ are in your brain but your intuition is in your brain and your gut

key_insights.insight: The process for changing ingrained habits involves raising awareness, focused attention on triggers and consequences, and deliberate practice of new behaviors, but also requires accountability.
key_insights.quote: Step one is spot the pattern... next stage is deliberate practice...but the fourth factor is accountability because most people left to their own devices will give up on that process when it feels too hard.

key_insights.insight: A way to 'trick' your brain and build self-esteem is to reverse recurring negative thoughts with a positive affirmation.
key_insights.quote: usually there's a particular recurring negative thought that's associated with feelings of lack of confidence um so if you can identify what that is and create a positive affirmation that's like the opposite of it or something that counteracts it then that can be a great way to get started.

key_insights.insight: If you want to manifest something into your life you have to not only make a list of the attributes you want in that person, but also be those things yourself.
key_insights.quote: I think the preferred method is to create a list of the attributes that you want in that person.But you then have to make sure that you are everything that is on that list.

key_insights.insight: A simple first step to moving toward change is to spend 5 minutes visualizing success and feeling gratitude.
key_insights.quote: spend five minutes sitting down and visualizing those things being true and then give gratitude for that that would be my first step

actionable_advice.advice: Look into someone's left eye with your right eye to trigger innate bonding responses.
actionable_advice.how_to: Consciously focus your gaze on the other person's left eye, and try to make eye contact before starting a conversation. Do this when meeting someone to create a more innate bond.

actionable_advice.advice: If you wake up in the middle of the night, turn onto your side to facilitate brain cleansing.
actionable_advice.how_to: Purchase a pillow that supports side sleeping if you are naturally a back sleeper, and if you wake up, ensure that you get on your side. Any side will do.

actionable_advice.advice: Combat a recurring negative thought by creating a positive affirmation that opposes it and repeat it regularly.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify recurring negative thoughts (e.g., 'I have to do everything perfectly'), reframe it into a positive affirmation (e.g., 'It doesn't have to be perfect to be great'), and then repeat that phrase.

actionable_advice.advice: Practice aesthetic appreciation to signal safety and support brain health.
actionable_advice.how_to: Fill your home with things that are beautiful, add objects of beauty, or simply put things in place where they smell nice in the morning. Get in touch with the beautiful side of life and aesthetics that may not currently exist in your surroundings or home.

actionable_advice.advice: To help manage your stress if you have particularly stressful experiences, introduce meditation, and find books and videos for children regarding emotional education.
actionable_advice.how_to: Start by understanding if your child is going through a particularly stressful situation. If so, find ways for both you and the child to manage that stress and educate yourselves regarding emotions.

memorable_stories: The CEO's PA shouting 'He's the reason I'm fat!' due to the CEO's stress leaking down to the team.
An experiment in which octogenarians lived in retrofitted homes and 'reverted' to their younger selves, resulting in improved posture and musculoskeletal coordination.
Young medical students slowed down after walking between rooms with the words 'Florida, beach, sunshine, walk, bungalow' printed on paper, because those words were associated with retirement.
contrarian_claims: Co-sleeping is fundamental to emotional and spiritual survival, so sleep divorce should be avoided.
There is no such thing as 'good stress'.
A kiss in the morning can affect a menstrual cycle.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - No.1 Neuroscientist: Why You Should Always Look Into Someone’s Left Eye! & How Stress Leaks Through Skin, Is Contagious & Gives You Belly Fat! Dr. Tara Swart
